name: Maryland Rusks
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the yeast with 2 cups of flour.
2. Heat the milk, melted butter, sugar, and salt until warm.
3. Mix the flour and milk mixture with the eggs and beat for half a minute on low speed, scraping the bowl. Then beat on high speed for 3 minutes.
4. Add enough flour to make a moderately stiff dough and knead until smooth (5-8 minutes by hand).
5. Shape into a ball and place in a greased bowl. Cover and let rise until doubled in bulk, about 1 1/2 hours.
6. Punch down the dough and divide into 5 parts. Cover and let rest for 10 minutes.
7. Roll each part into a 5-inch circle. Place on a greased baking sheet and cover. Let rise until doubled, about 30-45 minutes.
8. Brush with milk and sprinkle with sugar if desired.
9. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit (175 degrees Celsius) for 15-20 minutes or until golden.
10. Cool completely.
11. Slice the breads in half to form 2 thin circles. Place on a baking sheet and toast for 40 minutes at 275 degrees Fahrenheit (135 degrees Celsius), turning over after 20 minutes or until golden.
